spec_match.rationale: Correctly matches the current Captured Surface Control draft: `CaptureController.forwardWheel(element)` and zoom methods are exposed, while arbitrary click and keyboard forwarding are shown as blocked/nonexistent surfaces instead of being called.

interactive.rationale: The page has a live capture button, ghost cursor, normalized coordinate readout, wheel logging, focusable preview, keyboard logging, and blocked-action buttons for click and shortcut paths.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index now describes the concept as a safety lab for ghost-cursor coordinates, wheel forwarding, zoom availability, and blocked click/key passthrough rather than promising unsupported APIs.

edge_cases.rationale: No-capture state is explicit in the wheel log, feature detection reports unavailable APIs, and blocked paths cover click forwarding, Ctrl+T, Ctrl+W, Alt+Tab, and preview keydown handling.

references.rationale: The concept includes the required `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5092615678066688` link and the W3C draft reference in `see also`.

design_system.rationale: Local CSS uses shared variables and sets color alongside custom button backgrounds through inherited global button styling; no raw hex colors were added.

summary: Remote Cursor now reflects the actual Captured Surface Control API surface: wheel forwarding and zoom support are identified, click/key passthrough is treated as intentionally blocked, coordinate accuracy is visible, and the required ChromeStatus/spec references and availability banner are present.
